verses.latin_vulgate: Est et aliud malum quod vidi sub sole,  et quidem frequens apud homines :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יֵ֣שׁ רָעָ֔ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר רָאִ֖יתִי תַּ֣חַת הַשָּׁ֑מֶשׁ וְרַבָּ֥ה הִ֖יא עַל־הָאָדָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: There is also another evil, which I have seen under the sun, and that frequent among men:
verses.finnish_biblia: On onnettomuus tämäkin, jonka olen nähnyt auringon alla ja joka raskaasti painaa ihmistä:
verses.king_james: There is an evil which I have seen under the sun, and it is common among men:

verses.latin_vulgate: vir cui dedit Deus divitias,  et substantiam, et honorem,  et nihil deest animæ suæ ex omnibus quæ desiderat ;  nec tribuit ei potestatem Deus ut comedat ex eo,  sed homo extraneus vorabit illud :  hoc vanitas et miseria magna est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִ֣ישׁ אֲשֶׁ֣ר יִתֶּן־ל֣וֹ הָאֱלֹהִ֡ים עֹ֩שֶׁר֩ וּנְכָסִ֨ים וְכָב֜וֹד וְֽאֵינֶ֨נּוּ חָסֵ֥ר לְנַפְשׁ֣וֹ ׀ מִכֹּ֣ל אֲשֶׁר־יִתְאַוֶּ֗ה וְלֹֽא־יַשְׁלִיטֶ֤נּוּ הָֽאֱלֹהִים֙ לֶאֱכֹ֣ל מִמֶּ֔נּוּ כִּ֛י אִ֥ישׁ נׇכְרִ֖י יֹֽאכְלֶ֑נּוּ זֶ֥ה הֶ֛בֶל וׇחֳלִ֥י רָ֖ע הֽוּא׃
verses.douay_rheims: A man to whom God hath given riches, and substance, and honour, and his soul wanteth nothing of all that he desireth: yet God doth not give him power to eat thereof, but a stranger shall eat it up. This is vanity and a great misery.
verses.finnish_biblia: että Jumala antaa miehelle rikkautta ja tavaraa ja kunniaa, niin ettei hänen sielultaan puutu mitään kaikesta siitä, mitä hän halajaa, mutta Jumala ei salli hänen nauttia sitä, vaan sen nauttii vieras. Se on turhuutta ja raskas kärsimys.
verses.king_james: A man to whom God hath given riches, wealth, and honour, so that he wanteth nothing for his soul of all that he desireth, yet God giveth him not power to eat thereof, but a stranger eateth it: this is vanity, and it is an evil disease.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si genuerit quispiam centum liberos,  et vixerit multos annos,  et plures dies ætatis habuerit,  et anima illius non utatur bonis substantiæ suæ,  sepulturaque careat :  de hoc ergo pronuntio quod melior illo sit abortivus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־יוֹלִ֣יד אִ֣ישׁ מֵאָ֡ה וְשָׁנִים֩ רַבּ֨וֹת יִֽחְיֶ֜ה וְרַ֣ב ׀ שֶׁיִּהְי֣וּ יְמֵֽי־שָׁנָ֗יו וְנַפְשׁוֹ֙ לֹא־תִשְׂבַּ֣ע מִן־הַטּוֹבָ֔ה וְגַם־קְבוּרָ֖ה לֹא־הָ֣יְתָה לּ֑וֹ אָמַ֕רְתִּי ט֥וֹב מִמֶּ֖נּוּ הַנָּֽפֶל׃
verses.douay_rheims: If a man beget a hundred children, and live many years, and attain to a great age, and his soul make no use of the goods of his substance, and he be without burial: of this man I pronounce, that the untimely born is better than he.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vaikka syntyisi miehelle sata lasta ja hän eläisi vuosia paljon ja paljot olisivat hänen vuottensa päivät, mutta hän ei saisi tyydyttää omaa haluaan omaisuudellansa eikä saisi edes hautaustakaan, niin minä sanon, että keskoinen olisi onnellisempi kuin hän.
verses.king_james: If a man beget an hundred children, and live many years, so that the days of his years be many, and his soul be not filled with good, and also that he have no burial; I say, that an untimely birth is better than he.

verses.latin_vulgate: Frustra enim venit,  et pergit ad tenebras,  et oblivione delebitur nomen ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־בַהֶ֥בֶל בָּ֖א וּבַחֹ֣שֶׁךְ יֵלֵ֑ךְ וּבַחֹ֖שֶׁךְ שְׁמ֥וֹ יְכֻסֶּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: For he came in vain, and goeth to darkness, and his name shall be wholly forgotten.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä se turhaan tulee ja pimeyteen menee, ja pimeyteen peittyy sen nimi.
verses.king_james: For he cometh in with vanity, and departeth in darkness, and his name shall be covered with darkness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non vidit solem,  neque cognovit distantiam boni et mali.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּם־שֶׁ֥מֶשׁ לֹא־רָאָ֖ה וְלֹ֣א יָדָ֑ע נַ֥חַת לָזֶ֖ה מִזֶּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: He hath not seen the sun, nor known the distance of good and evil:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ei se ole aurinkoa nähnyt eikä tuntenut. Sen lepo on parempi kuin hänen.
verses.king_james: Moreover he hath not seen the sun, nor known any thing: this hath more rest than the other.

verses.latin_vulgate: Etiam si duobus millibus annis vixerit,  et non fuerit perfruitus bonis,  nonne ad unum locum properant omnia ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאִלּ֣וּ חָיָ֗ה אֶ֤לֶף שָׁנִים֙ פַּעֲמַ֔יִם וְטוֹבָ֖ה לֹ֣א רָאָ֑ה הֲלֹ֛א אֶל־מָק֥וֹם אֶחָ֖ד הַכֹּ֥ל הוֹלֵֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Although he lived two thousand years, and hath not enjoyed good things: do not all make haste to one place?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja vaikka hän eläisi kaksi kertaa tuhannen vuotta, mutta ei saisi onnea nähdä - eikö kuitenkin kaikki mene samaan paikkaan?
verses.king_james: Yea, though he live a thousand years twice told, yet hath he seen no good: do not all go to one place?

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnis labor hominis in ore ejus ;  sed anima ejus non implebitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כׇּל־עֲמַ֥ל הָאָדָ֖ם לְפִ֑יהוּ וְגַם־הַנֶּ֖פֶשׁ לֹ֥א תִמָּלֵֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: All the labour of man is for his mouth, but his soul shall not be filled.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kaikki ihmisen vaivannäkö tapahtuu hänen oman suunsa hyväksi, ja kuitenkaan ei halu täyty.
verses.king_james: All the labour of man is for his mouth, and yet the appetite is not filled.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid habet amplius sapiens a stulto ?  et quid pauper, nisi ut pergat illuc ubi est vita ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֛י מַה־יּוֹתֵ֥ר לֶחָכָ֖ם מִֽן־הַכְּסִ֑יל מַה־לֶּעָנִ֣י יוֹדֵ֔עַ לַהֲלֹ֖ךְ נֶ֥גֶד הַחַיִּֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: What hath the wise man more than the fool? and what the poor man, but to go thither, where there is life?
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä mitä etua on viisaalla tyhmän edellä, ja mitä kurjalla siitä, että hän osaa oikein vaeltaa elävitten edessä?
verses.king_james: For what hath the wise more than the fool? what hath the poor, that knoweth to walk before the living?

verses.latin_vulgate: Melius est videre quod cupias,  quam desiderare quod nescias.  Sed et hoc vanitas est, et præsumptio spiritus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ט֛וֹב מַרְאֵ֥ה עֵינַ֖יִם מֵֽהֲלׇךְ־נָ֑פֶשׁ גַּם־זֶ֥ה הֶ֖בֶל וּרְע֥וּת רֽוּחַ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Better it is to see what thou mayst desire, than to desire that which thou canst not know. But this also is vanity, and presumption of spirit.
verses.finnish_biblia: Parempi silmän näkö kuin halun haihattelu. Tämäkin on turhuutta ja tuulen tavoittelua.
verses.king_james: Better is the sight of the eyes than the wandering of the desire: this is also vanity and vexation of spirit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui futurus est, jam vocatum est nomen ejus ;  et scitur quod homo sit,  et non possit contra fortiorem se in judicio contendere.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַה־שֶּֽׁהָיָ֗ה כְּבָר֙ נִקְרָ֣א שְׁמ֔וֹ וְנוֹדָ֖ע אֲשֶׁר־ה֣וּא אָדָ֑ם וְלֹא־יוּכַ֣ל לָדִ֔ין עִ֥ם (שהתקיף) [שֶׁתַּקִּ֖יף] מִמֶּֽנּוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: He that shall be, his name is already called: and it is known, that he is a man, and cannot contend in judgment with him that is stronger than himself.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mitä olemassa on, sille on pantu nimi jo ammoin; ja edeltä tunnettua on ollut, mitä ihmisestä on tuleva. Ei voi hän riidellä väkevämpänsä kanssa.
verses.king_james: That which hath been is named already, and it is known that it is man: neither may he contend with him that is mightier than he.

verses.latin_vulgate: Verba sunt plurima,  multamque in disputando habentia vanitatem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֛י יֵשׁ־דְּבָרִ֥ים הַרְבֵּ֖ה מַרְבִּ֣ים הָ֑בֶל מַה־יֹּתֵ֖ר לָאָדָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: There are many words that have much vanity in disputing.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä niin on: puheen paljous enentää turhuutta. Mitä etua on ihmisellä siitä?
verses.king_james: Seeing there be many things that increase vanity, what is man the better?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid necesse est homini majora se quærere,  cum ignoret quid conducat sibi in vita sua,  numero dierum peregrinationis suæ,  et tempore quod velut umbra præterit ?  aut quis ei poterit indicare  quod post eum futurum sub sole sit ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י מִֽי־יוֹדֵ֩עַ֩ מַה־טּ֨וֹב לָֽאָדָ֜ם בַּֽחַיִּ֗ים מִסְפַּ֛ר יְמֵי־חַיֵּ֥י הֶבְל֖וֹ וְיַעֲשֵׂ֣ם כַּצֵּ֑ל אֲשֶׁר֙ מִֽי־יַגִּ֣יד לָֽאָדָ֔ם מַה־יִּהְיֶ֥ה אַחֲרָ֖יו תַּ֥חַת הַשָּֽׁמֶשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: What needeth a man to seek things that are above him, whereas he knoweth not what is profitable for him in his life, in all the days of his pilgrimage, and the time that passeth like a shadow? Or who can tell him what shall be after him under the sun?
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä kuka tietää, mikä on ihmiselle hyvä elämässä, hänen elämänsä lyhyinä, turhina päivinä, jotka hän viettää kuin varjo; ja kuka ilmaisee ihmiselle, mitä on tuleva hänen jälkeensä auringon alla?
verses.king_james: For who knoweth what is good for man in this life, all the days of his vain life which he spendeth as a shadow? for who can tell a man what shall be after him under the sun?
